question_title: Will WHO publish at least one new Disease Outbreak News item about cholera or acute watery diarrhoea before April 30, 2026?
summary: The question asks whether WHO will publish at least one Disease Outbreak News (DON) item about cholera or acute watery diarrhoea between March 17 and April 30, 2026. As of March 31, 14 days have passed with no such DON. The last cholera DON was published August 29, 2025 (7 months ago). WHO has structurally shifted cholera reporting from DONs to monthly epidemiological updates/situation reports (#33-#35), published under /publications/m/item/ rather than the DON page. In 2024, zero cholera DONs were published despite an active global outbreak. In 2025, only 2 were published. The Poisson base rate for a 30-day window is ~7-15%. However, the cholera situation remains severe (18,700+ cases/month, Southern Africa 7x surge, DRC epidemic expanding), which could trigger a new DON. The most recent epidemiological update (#35) was published March 27 as a situation report, not a DON, suggesting WHO continues its non-DON approach. I estimate ~30% probability, above the pure base rate due to severity and potential triggers, but well below 50% given the clear structural shift in WHO's communication pattern.

factors.description: Historical base rate: ~2 cholera DONs per year in 2025, ~0 in 2024. Over a 30-day remaining window, Poisson P(>=1) is 7-15%.
WHO structural shift to monthly epidemiological updates (#33-#35) instead of DONs for cholera, reducing DON publication frequency significantly since 2023
14 days of resolution window already passed (Mar 17-31) with no cholera DON published
WHO published epidemiological update #35 on Mar 27 as a situation report (not DON), suggesting continuation of non-DON approach for cholera
Active severe cholera situation: 18,715 cases in Feb 2026, Southern Africa 7x surge, DRC epidemic expanding, 269 deaths with 48% monthly increase
7-month gap since last cholera DON (Aug 2025) - WHO has historically published periodic global cholera DONs
Southern Africa acute surge due to cyclones/flooding is a NEW development that could warrant a country/regional DON (similar to Angola DON in 2025)
2024 had zero cholera DONs despite active global outbreak, showing WHO can go a full year without cholera DONs

reasoning:
**Forecast: 30% probability (YES)**

Key reasoning:
- **Base rate is low**: Only 2 cholera DONs in the last 27 months (2024-2026), giving a Poisson probability of ~7-15% for a 30-day window
- **Structural shift**: WHO has moved cholera reporting from DONs to monthly epidemiological situation reports (#33-#35), published under a different URL path that doesn't count per resolution criteria
- **14 days already passed**: No cholera DON published since March 17 despite active outbreaks
- **Update #35 published March 27 as situation report, not DON**: WHO chose the non-DON format just 4 days ago
- **Upside risk**: Severe ongoing situation (18,700+ monthly cases, Southern Africa 7x surge, DRC epidemic) could trigger a new DON, and the 7-month gap since the last one is notable
sources_consulted: [Cholera – Multi-country with a focus on countries experiencing current surges](https://www.who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news/item/2025-DON579)
[(Metaculus) Will WHO publish at least one new Disease Outbreak News item about cholera or acute watery diarrhoea before April 30, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42929)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if WHO publishes at least one new Disease Outbreak News item with the title mentioning cholera or diarrhoea on or after March 17, 2026 and before May 1, 2026, using the WHO Disease Outbreak News page at https://www.who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news. It resolves to no otherwise.
fine_print: Count only Disease Outbreak News items, not other WHO news posts, situation reports, or regional office pages unless they are linked as Disease Outbreak News items. Candidate Sources: WHO cholera upsurge page https://www.who.int/emergencies/situations/cholera-upsurge
